description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped stone with a rugged, angular form and a dark, earthy coloration. The surface is coarse and uneven, featuring a mix of jagged edges and shallow depressions, with some areas showing subtle reddish-brown and gray mottling. A small, metallic marker or label is visible on one face, suggesting it may have been cataloged or studied. The stone's texture is predominantly rough, with visible pitting and minor weathering, indicating exposure to natural elements. There are no visible carvings or artificial modifications aside from the label. Its overall appearance suggests it could serve as a natural obstacle or decorative element in an urban park or landscaped area.
description: A rugged, dark stone with an irregular, angular shape and coarse, weathered surface, featuring a small metallic label on one side.
description_view_0: This view highlights the stone's largest, flattest face with a visible metallic label and some subtle reddish-brown mottling.
description_view_1: This angle shows a more rounded, less angular side with a darker, more uniform coloration and fewer visible surface features.
description_view_2: This perspective emphasizes the stone's jagged, protruding edge and the deep shadowed recess beneath it.
description_view_3: This view presents a sloped, rough surface with the metallic label just visible at the top, and less pronounced color variation.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or obstacle on the ground
attribute_pattern: Irregular mottling with subtle reddish and gray patches, no regular or artificial patterns.
attribute_weathering: Rough and pitted with minor surface weathering, indicating moderate exposure to outdoor elements.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with paved or manicured surfaces, but could blend with natural landscaping or other stones nearby.
